diagrams.title: Normalization Hierarchy
diagrams.description: Shows the progression from 1NF to BCNF with conditions at each level
elements: 3NF: No transitive dependencies (or right-side is prime)
diagrams.purpose: Understand the relationship between different normal forms and remember BCNF is stricter than 3NF

diagrams.title: BCNF Decomposition Process
diagrams.description: Visual representation of how a relation is decomposed into BCNF relations
elements: Original relation R with violating FD X→Y
Repeat until all relations in BCNF
Check each new relation for violations
diagrams.purpose: Visualize the step-by-step decomposition algorithm for BCNF

diagrams.title: Superkey Hierarchy
diagrams.description: Shows relationship between superkeys, candidate keys, and primary keys
elements: Outer circle: Superkeys (all attribute sets that determine relation)
Inner point: Primary key (chosen candidate key)
diagrams.purpose: Understand the difference between these key concepts essential for BCNF determination

diagrams.title: 3NF vs BCNF Venn Diagram
diagrams.description: Shows which relations satisfy each normal form
elements: Region outside smallest: Relations in BCNF but not violating 3NF
diagrams.purpose: Visualize that BCNF violations are a subset of 3NF violations, meaning BCNF is stricter
